name: Basis Theory
description: Processor-agnostic payment tokenization API letting merchants control sensitive transaction data.

seo.title: Basis Theory — Payments Orchestration
seo.meta_description: Basis Theory: Processor-agnostic payment tokenization API letting merchants control sensitive transaction data.

when_to_use: Tokenizing payment card data to reduce PCI DSS scope
Migrating cardholder data between PSPs without re-collecting card info
Storing raw PANs in a vault while routing tokens to multiple acquirers
Building processor-agnostic checkout with shared token vault

pros: Processor-agnostic tokens portable across any PSP
Clean developer docs and strong SDKs
Reduces PCI scope to SAQ A without vendor lock-in
Good fit for multi-PSP and reseller architectures
cons: Tokenization only — no routing or acquiring
Requires you to build the orchestration layer on top
Pricing scales with tokens and API calls
Less value if you are happy on one PSP's vault
review.verdict: Best-in-class portable card vault for teams building their own orchestration — not a full payments platform.
review.best_for: Engineering teams that want PCI offload and PSP portability without being locked into Stripe or Adyen vaults.
review.not_for: Merchants wanting a full routing, reconciliation, and analytics product out of the box.
